food_name: BBQ Chicken Wings (Hawker-Style, Soy and Honey Marinade, Charcoal or Gas Grilled)

local_names.romanized_local: Kǎo Jī Chì (Chinese); Sayap Ayam BBQ (Malay)

food_subgroup: Chicken wings (mid-joint and/or drumette), skin-on, marinated in soy sauce, dark soy sauce, honey, sesame oil and five-spice powder, grilled over charcoal or gas

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Singapore-style hawker BBQ chicken wings. Closest parent concept is grilled chicken (FOODON:03302757). No exact URI assigned as the Singapore-specific preparation with soy-honey marinade is not represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, grilled — FoodEx2 closest parent for grilled chicken preparation. Singapore-specific marinade not represented; closest_parent match applied.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A039V (Chicken, grilled) is the closest defensible parent. The soy-honey marinade and charcoal/gas grilling method specific to Singapore hawker preparation are not individually coded in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Singapore-style BBQ chicken wings as a prepared food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker serving of BBQ chicken wings is 3 pieces (mid-joint and/or drumettes including bone), weighing approximately 210g total, yielding approximately 120g of edible meat and skin after bone removal.

nutrient_source.title: BBQ Chicken Wings — Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / Foodo nutritional data; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for chicken wing, meat and skin, cooked, roasted (NDB 05072)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / Foodo; USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database for BBQ chicken wings (hawker-style). Values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05072 (chicken wing, meat and skin, roasted) for secondary nutrient plausibility. Sodium elevated relative to plain roasted chicken due to soy sauce marinade, consistent with HPB data. All values represent edible portion (meat and skin, bone excluded).

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of protein- and fat-dominant foods — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference data for grilled chicken and low-carbohydrate meat preparations
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI test exists for Singapore hawker BBQ chicken wings. GI estimated at approximately 15 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database reference data for grilled chicken and similar protein-fat-dominant preparations with minimal carbohydrate. The soy-honey marinade contributes approximately 3–4g carbohydrate per 100g edible, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 0.7 per 3-wing serving (120g edible). This is consistent with the GI Database classification of plain grilled chicken as low-GI. Estimation basis: protein and fat dominance suppresses glycemic response; honey-derived sugar is a minor contributor at hawker serving quantities.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium from soy sauce and dark soy sauce marinade (380 mg/100g; approximately 456 mg per 3-wing serving of 120g edible)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit BBQ chicken wings to one serving (3 pieces) per meal and avoid additional high-sodium condiments or dishes in the same meal, as the cumulative sodium from soy-based marinades can contribute meaningfully to the 1500–2000 mg daily sodium target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic load (~0.7 per serving); minimal carbohydrate (4g/100g, predominantly from honey in marinade); high protein (18g/100g) supports satiety and glycemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: BBQ chicken wings are a suitable hawker protein choice for patients with Type 2 Diabetes: the grilled preparation adds no batter or frying oil, carbohydrate content is minimal, and the high protein content supports postprandial glycemic control — patients should pair with a low-GI carbohydrate side and monitor portion size.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from chicken skin (4.5g/100g) and moderate sodium (380 mg/100g); cholesterol 90 mg/100g; grilled preparation avoids additional frying oils
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease may consume BBQ chicken wings in moderation (1 serving, 3 pieces); removing the skin reduces saturated fat intake by approximately 30–40%, and the grilled preparation is preferable to fried alternatives, but sodium from the soy marinade should be factored into daily cardiovascular risk management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chicken skin contains arachidonic acid (omega-6 precursor to pro-inflammatory eicosanoids); heterocyclic amines from high-temperature charcoal grilling may have inflammatory properties at high intake
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Chicken wings are a lean, easily digestible protein source suitable for most patients with rheumatoid arthritis at normal dietary intake levels; patients with high inflammatory burden may consider removing the skin and limiting charcoal-grilled preparations to occasional consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: BBQ Chicken Wings (烤鸡翅, Sayap Ayam BBQ) are a universally beloved Singapore hawker staple: chicken wings — typically mid-joint and drumette pieces — marinated overnight in a caramelising blend of soy sauce, dark soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, and five-spice powder, then grilled over charcoal or gas until the skin is crispy, smoky, and lightly charred, with a deeply savoury-sweet glaze. Available at Malay, Chinese, and general hawker stalls throughout Singapore, they are a go-to supper food and hawker side dish, commonly served at BBQ stalls and hawker centres island-wide. From a clinical nutrition perspective, BBQ chicken wings are one of the more favourable hawker protein choices: the grilled preparation adds no batter or frying oil, carbohydrate content is minimal at approximately 4g per 100g edible (the honey marinade contributes only ~3g sugar per 100g), protein is high at 18g per 100g edible, and fat is primarily from the skin. The moderate sodium content of 380mg per 100g — derived from soy sauce and dark soy sauce in the marinade — is the primary clinical concern for hypertension patients. For patients with Type 2 Diabetes, BBQ chicken wings represent a near-ideal hawker protein side dish with a glycemic load of approximately 0.7 per 3-wing serving. Saturated fat at 4.5g per 100g is moderate; skin removal reduces this substantially. A standard hawker serving is 3 pieces (mid-joint and/or drumettes) weighing approximately 210g total, yielding approximately 120g of edible meat and skin.

confidence_reason: Nutritional values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (primary approved source for Singapore hawker dishes) and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for chicken wing, meat and skin, roasted. Values are internally consistent and plausible for a soy-honey marinated grilled chicken wing preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference data, clearly documented as such.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Singapore hawker BBQ chicken wings (soy-honey marinade, charcoal/gas grilled) is not separately reported in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05072 at the level of individual fatty acid fractions for this specific preparation. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this specific hawker preparation is not reported in HPB Foodo. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 per HPB source data; confirmed negligible for grilled chicken preparations. vitamin_c_mg reported as 0.0: chicken meat and skin contain negligible vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and HPB data. dietary_fibre_g reported as 0.0: naturally zero for a meat-only preparation with no plant-based ingredients in significant quantity. GI value of 15 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference data for grilled chicken and protein-fat-dominant preparations; no direct GI test exists for Singapore hawker BBQ chicken wings. FoodEx2 code A039V (Chicken, grilled) applied as closest_parent; Singapore-specific soy-honey marinade preparation is not individually coded. All per_100g values represent the edible portion (meat and skin, bone excluded). Standard serving: 3 pieces (210g total including bone, ~120g edible).
